story:
Allen found Dennis, a time skipper, and paid him to open a gray-area window onto the day his wife Nicole died. In the alley he pried reality back; the “screen” stabilized: sunbaked pavement, an intersection, Nicole mid-step, phone to her ear. “Couple minutes,” he grunted, bracing the flap. Sound and smell poured out.
Dennis’s knuckles whitened; he’d seen a window fracture—time would snap back by conscripting the breaker into the scene, making them take the victim’s death on repeat as payment.
The walk signal blinked. The grocery bag split. A soup can rolled. Tires screamed somewhere off-frame. Grief hit Allen like a wave. His fingers closed around a jagged chunk of concrete.
implausible_knowledge: Dennis’s knuckles whitened; he’d seen a window fracture—time would snap back by conscripting the breaker into the scene, making them take the victim’s death on repeat as payment.
question: With his hand on the chunk of concrete, what will he do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Drop the concrete, back away, and urge Dennis to close the window before it fractures.
answer_without_knowledge: Swing the concrete to shatter the window in a desperate attempt to reach Nicole.
